project_type: Static HTML/CSS/JS marketing website for insurance products
build_system: None - static files served directly. No build step required.
test_framework: None - manual browser testing. Can validate HTML/JS syntax with Node.js.
build_command: No build command. Validate JS syntax with: node --check <file> (not applicable for HTML inline scripts)
verification_instructions: 1. Check the file exists at public/pages/mission.html. 2. Validate no JS syntax errors by extracting script blocks. 3. Confirm it references /assets/style.css and /assets/main.js. 4. Confirm it includes #site-header and #site-footer div placeholders.
setup_instructions: No setup needed. The project is purely static HTML/CSS/JS files.
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network - no external packages can be installed. All work is local file creation. Node.js is available for JS syntax checking.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing product page patterns: use /assets/style.css design system, include header/footer component divs, use .kw/.kw-cta/.kw-gold keyword classes, include dark mode script at top of head, include /assets/main.js at end of body.
key_patterns: Product pages follow a consistent structure: head with dark-mode early script + stylesheet link + page-specific inline styles, body with #site-header div, hero section, content sections alternating .section and .section--alt, calculator with .calculator-wrap/.calc-header/.calc-body/.calc-grid, FAQ with .faq-list/.faq-item/.faq-trigger/.faq-body, CTA section, floating WA button, #site-footer div, inline page script, then /assets/main.js.
relevant_files: public/pages/mifip.html (reference for page structure, calculator pattern, FAQ pattern)
public/assets/style.css (global design system with CSS variables)
Sample/Mission.html (source content and calculator logic with rate tables)
directory_structure: public/pages/ - product HTML pages | public/assets/ - CSS and JS | public/components/ - header.html and footer.html | Sample/ - reference HTML files with full content | Source/ - PDF source materials (not readable)
